p0Mechanisms.why: Current DSXU evidence already shows lower cost and higher cache hit without score regression. Do not rebuild; harden as query-loop invariant.
Large measured prompt/tool-result reduction. Expand acceptance across Read/Bash/PowerShell/MCP/Agent, but keep source-truth fallback.
Parsing overhead is negligible and existing repair routing passes. Additional repair types need a bounded repair smoke proving fewer failed tool turns without open-ended retries.
Flash-first already saves cost. Only add failure-signal ledger; do not increase Pro usage unless pass-after-escalation improves.

blockingRisksFound.interpretation: Not a Reasonix P0 proof. Failures concentrate in PowerShell timeout paths and an archived provider-migration env-string expectation. Do not use the broad suite as a green signal until those owner issues are closed.

blockingRisksFound.result: PASS_VISIBLE_STATE_ACCEPTANCE with blocked guard: side-effect tool path has blocked permission state
blockingRisksFound.interpretation: Projection works, but real TUI/permission path still needs resize/permission prompt regression coverage before public experience claim.

absorptionRule: Do not implement a P0 mechanism unless the same-task smoke keeps quality/score stable and improves at least one measured vector: cost, cacheHitRatePct, toolResultChars, failedToolTurnCount, wallClockMs, or ProAdmissionCount.
